findings.message: N+1 query: `get_item_names` issues one SELECT per item ID — first fetches all IDs with `select(Item.id)` (unbounded, no LIMIT), then calls `session.get(Item, iid)` in a loop, producing N+1 round-trips to the database for every request. On a table with many rows this degrades linearly and can saturate the connection pool.
findings.suggestion: Replace the loop with a single column query: `return list(session.scalars(select(Item.name)))`. If the table is large, add pagination (LIMIT/OFFSET) consistent with the existing `list_items` pattern.
